steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes the tree-planting approach. The colon after the blank signals that what follows is proof: the Azores bullfinch population grew from as few as 100 birds to around 1,300 in 2023 — a 13-fold increase. The word must match that evidence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘successful’: Means achieving the intended result. The intended result was to boost bird numbers, and the population did grow dramatically. The evidence directly confirms this word. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘disastrous’: Means causing severe harm or failure. The population grew — the opposite of a disaster. Direct contradiction of the evidence. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘amusing’: Means entertaining or funny. Conservation programs are not described as a source of amusement, and the evidence (population growth data) has nothing to do with humor. Unsupported. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘costly’: Means expensive. The passage gives no information about the financial cost of the tree-planting program. The colon introduces population numbers, not budget figures. Unsupported. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
